summary: President D. Todd Christofferson conducted a solemn assembly in which Church members worldwide sustained President Dallin H. Oaks as prophet, seer, revelator, and President of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, along with his counselors and the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles.
haiku:
Hands rise in covenant
Saints sustain the Lord’s servants
Faith joins hearts as one
key_points: A solemn assembly is a sacred occasion for the united sustaining of Church leaders.
Members sustain prophets, seers, and revelators by uplifted hand and by faithful support.
The worldwide Church acts in order and unity under priesthood keys and prophetic leadership.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Doctrine and Covenants 20:65–67 — common consent and ordination in the Church
Doctrine and Covenants 26:2 — all things shall be done by common consent in the Church
Doctrine and Covenants 107:22 — the First Presidency sustained by the confidence, faith, and prayers of the Church
Doctrine and Covenants 21:4–6 — receiving the prophet’s words in patience and faith
Doctrine and Covenants 1:38 — whether by the Lord’s voice or His servants’ voice, it is the same
The principle of sustaining leaders through faith, prayer, and covenant loyalty
The doctrine of prophets, seers, and revelators holding priesthood keys to guide the Church
questions_to_ponder: What does it mean to truly sustain a prophet beyond raising my hand?
How can I increase my faith and prayers in behalf of those called to lead the Church?
Why is common consent important in the Lord’s Church?
How does a solemn assembly help members feel unity with Saints throughout the world?
What blessings come from following living prophets, seers, and revelators?
